riskFactors: GFI is a protocol/governance token, not a direct redeemable claim on a specific RWA reserve asset, credit portfolio, fund share, or collateral pool.
Goldfinch Prime product documentation describes private-credit exposure and Heron-related note context, but that product-level structure should not be treated as native GFI token backing.
No public legal opinion URL, GFI-specific prospectus, GFI-specific SEC filing, or directly verified regulator mapping was found during this research pass.
No on-chain proof-of-reserves oracle, reserve attestation, reserve breakdown, custodian, redemption asset, or native GFI collateralization ratio was identified.
GFI token contract is public and source-code verified on Etherscan, but no Etherscan-submitted security audit or official external audit report was confirmed in this pass.
Market data is available from CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, DeFiLlama, and Etherscan, but values conflict materially across aggregators and must be refreshed before production grading.
Liquidity is mostly secondary-market trading. DeFiLlama snapshot shows modest onchain DEX liquidity and higher CEX-driven volume; bid-ask spread and exchange-level order-book depth were not verified.
Holder count is visible on Etherscan, but top-wallet concentration and labeled-wallet exposure have not yet been analyzed.
mitigants: Goldfinch has official website, official docs, official FAQ, governance forum, legacy app, official GitHub repository, and public legal terms pages available for research.
GFI contract address is publicly visible on Ethereum and can be independently verified through Etherscan.
CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, DeFiLlama, and Etherscan provide market, holder, liquidity, and protocol-level metrics that can be refreshed for ongoing monitoring.
The scoring keeps GFI separate from Goldfinch Prime, reducing the risk of incorrectly treating a protocol token as a reserve-backed private-credit product.
Protocol-level TVL, active-loan, fee, revenue, and liquidity references are available through DeFiLlama for context, even though they are not native GFI reserve backing.

scoreRationale.smartContractRisk: 55 because GFI is a public Ethereum ERC-20 with verified source code on Etherscan, but no confirmed official external audit report or Etherscan-submitted contract security audit was found.
scoreRationale.counterpartyRisk: 45 because GFI is not a direct claim on a specific reserve or private-credit portfolio; Goldfinch Prime introduces product-level Heron/private-credit context that still needs legal and regulator verification.
scoreRationale.liquidityRisk: 45 because GFI is publicly traded and has measurable liquidity, but onchain DEX liquidity appears modest, bid-ask spread is not verified, and there is no native redemption mechanism for GFI.
scoreRationale.regulatoryRisk: 40 because legal opinion, GFI-specific regulatory status, SEC/IAPD verification, and direct issuer mapping remain unresolved; Goldfinch Prime compliance terms should not be copied into GFI token compliance.
scoreRationale.marketRisk: 42 because GFI has live market data but remains a smaller-cap token with volatile aggregator values and requires close refresh before production publication.
scoreRationale.concentrationRisk: 50 because Etherscan holder count is available, but top-holder concentration, insider/team/treasury wallets, exchange wallets, and labeled-wallet distribution have not been analyzed.

_meta.gradingImplication: Risk score is not high enough for institutional grade. Combined with missing custodian, legal opinion, proof-of-reserves, redemption asset, formal audit evidence, and concentration analysis, goldfinch-gfi should remain research-grade until the missing evidence is resolved.
nextActions: Verify official external audit reports from Goldfinch docs, GitHub, governance, or security pages.
Analyze Etherscan holder distribution and label top wallets for concentration scoring.
Verify Heron Finance / Warbler Labs regulatory status through SEC IAPD and EDGAR before adjusting counterparty or regulatory scores.
Capture pair-specific DEX liquidity from GeckoTerminal or DEXScreener and CEX order-book depth before revising liquidityRisk.
Refresh CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, DeFiLlama, and Etherscan market metrics before import/grading.
Decide whether Goldfinch Prime should become a separate Nexus RWA asset entry, because its product-level risks and yield mechanics should not be merged into GFI token scoring.
